stories.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
stories.description: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.

stories.title: Trump unleashes curse-filled social media rant at Iran after U.S. rescues colonel
stories.description: In a profanity-laden post on Truth Social, President Trump lashed out at Iran and injected new volatility into the conflict, hours after U.S. forces carried out a high-risk rescue mission.

stories.title: U.S. searches for airman shot down in Iran, while Trump posts ultimatum
stories.description: At the start of the sixth week of war, U.S. forces search for a missing airman and President Trump reiterates his ultimatum for Iran to open the Straight of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: 'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The BBC's Orla Guerin travels to the edge of the critical waterway that Iran has put a stranglehold on.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: US president Donald Trump warns Tehran to reopen Hormuz or face "hell"
stories.description: Iran and the United States received a draft proposal late Sunday calling for a 45-day ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Donald Trump threatens to hit Iran power plants
stories.description: Israeli strikes killed the intelligence chief of Iran's Revolutionary Guards, as the Islamic republic on Monday defied threats from US President Donald Trump to devastate civilian infrastructure if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Could China's adoption of electric vehicles shield it from the energy crisis?
stories.description: As crude prices hover around the $110 per barrel mark on Monday morning, consumers worldwide are feeling the impact of the war in Iran and the closure of the Hormuz Strait when they head to the petrol pump. In China, where more than half of new car sales are for electric vehicles...

stories.title: 'Hit by both sides': US war on Iran threatens Iraq's 'careful strategy of multi-alignment'
stories.description: Catherine Viette is pleased to welcome Dr Renad Mansour, Deputy Director for the Middle East and North Africa Programme and Director of the Iraq Initiative at Chatham House. For years, Iraq has pursued a strategy of multi-alignment, explains Dr. Mansour, carefully maintaining rel...

stories.title: Middle East war: ‘No indication that legitimate US-Iran talks taking place’
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayad said that “everyone” in Iran is aware of US President Donald Trump’s deadline to reopen the strait of Hormuz, but regardless of what the White House might be saying “there’s no indication that legitimate talks are taking place” betwee...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran Revolutionary Guards’ intelligence chief Khademi killed in strikes
stories.description: The head of ‌the Revolutionary Guards’ intelligence ‌organisation, Majid Khademi, was on Monday killed in US-Israeli air strikes. Khademi “was martyred in the criminal terrorist attack by the American-Zionist enemy ... at dawn today", the Guards announced in a post on their Teleg...

stories.title: Trump hails victory after US mission to rescue pilot from Iran
stories.description: American commandos deployed deep into Iranian territory to rescue a downed airman, US news outlets reported on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump announced that the crew member had been recovered "safe and sound."

stories.title: War in the Middle East: Israel says residential building hit by Iranian missile
stories.description: 'Stuck in a warzone': France 24's Noga Tarnopolsky describes the plight of Israelis who are unable to evacuate from the north.

stories.title: Israel launches new strikes on Lebanon, forces closure of Syria border crossing
stories.description: Israeli airstrikes on Beirut and southern Lebanon on Sunday killed at least 11 people, including a family of six, and wounded dozens more. The attacks also prompted Syria to temporarily close its main border crossing with Lebanon, as Israel's conflict with Hezbollah intensifies.

stories.title: War in the Middle East: the US rescues missing airman from Iran
stories.description: Jim Townsend, Former US Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense fir European and NATO Policy, is our guest.

stories.title: US war on Iran: Trump pressures allies in war effort but offers 'no role in strategy'
stories.description: Kethevane Gorjestani is pleased to welcome Ian Lesser, Author and Senior Director for Foreign and Security Policy at the German Marshall Fund (GMF). According to Mr. Lesser, the current crisis in the Middle East has sparked a systemic shock to the global order. What we are witnes...

stories.title: Yemen’s civilians fear fallout after Houthis enter Iran war
stories.description: Fears of air strikes and rising prices dominate everyday life in Sanaa as Yemen becomes embroiled in regional conflict.

stories.title: Trump says US armed Iranian dissidents via Kurds, Kurdish groups deny claim
stories.description: Trump's admission gives credence to Iranian assertion that the January protests were backed by the West to create chaos.

stories.title: Iran’s top university bombed as US, Israel intensify attacks; 34 killed
stories.description: Tehran says it will respond 'in kind' to attacks on its infrastructure, accuses Trump of inciting 'war crimes'.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 38 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: Tensions continue after Trump's threat on Truth Social for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: A new regional order for the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The GCC and Iran can come up with a new security architecture for the Strait of Hormuz that can give Trump an off-ramp.

stories.title: Vietnam’s gig workers slammed by rising fuel costs amid fallout of Iran war
stories.description: Diesel prices have more than doubled in the Southeast Asian nation amid Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran rejects Trump’s Hormuz deadline; 2 killed in Haifa
stories.description: Iranian officials dismiss Trump's ultimatum, condemn latest threats as 'incitement to war crimes'.

stories.title: Has the humanitarian crisis in Gaza been ignored?
stories.description: Conditions in Gaza worsen amid the United States-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran in expletive-filled social media post
stories.description: Donald Trump unleashed a vulgar online tirade against Iran’s leaders as his deadline for reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Israel continues deadly attacks on Gaza while striking Iran, Lebanon
stories.description: Israel has kept up its attacks on Gaza while striking Iran and Lebanon, killing at least four Palestinians on Sunday.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz stays sticking point as ceasefire remains elusive
stories.description: • Trump warns power plants, bridges to be attacked on Tuesday, hopeful for Hormuz deal; Iran wants world to ‘act now’ to prevent ‘war crime’ • Iranian official says Hormuz strait will be opened only after payment of war damages • China, Russia call for ceasefire; Lavrov tells Was...

stories.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif ‘deeply’ concerned after 3 Pakistani nationals injured in UAE amid Iran conflict
stories.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Monday expressed concern over an incident at the Khor Fakkan port in the United Arab Emirates, where a missile interception injured three Pakistani nationals. Gulf countries, including the UAE, have come under attack in Iran’s retaliatory campaign...

stories.title: 11 killed in Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon
stories.description: JERUSALEM: An Israeli airstrike on Kfarhata, a village in south Lebanon, killed seven people on Sunday, including a 4-year-old child, Lebanon’s health ministry said in a statement. Another Israeli attack on the Jnah neighbourhood in Beirut killed four people and injured 39 others...

stories.title: AI at war: 5 things to know about Project Maven
stories.description: WASHINGTON: A Pentagon AI programme called Project Maven is at the centre of the US strikes against Iran and potentially one of the most consequential transformations of modern warfare. What is it? Project Maven is the Pentagon’s flagship artificial intelligence programme, launch...

stories.title: US forces rescue 2 airmen downed in Iran; Trump says crew member injured but ‘safe’
stories.description: United States President Donald Trump said on Sunday that US forces had safely recovered a second airman downed in Iran, calling it “one of the most daring search and rescue operations in US history”. The announcement came as Iran launched missiles and drones at Israel and Kuwait ...

stories.title: EU urged to drop sanctions on import of Russian oil and gas
stories.description: WARSAW: The European Union should end sanctions on Russian oil and gas imports, take steps to restore Druzhba oil pipeline flows and end the war in Ukraine to tackle the energy crisis stemming from the war in Iran, Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Saturday. Fico said in ...

stories.title: Iran conflict depletes Patriot, THAAD interceptor stocks — newspaper
stories.description: Dwindling interceptor stockpiles could leave US allies around the world vulnerable to attacks

stories.title: Saudi Arabia’s damage from US operation against Iran exceeds $10 bln — WSJ
stories.description: The country’s authorities and major state-owned companies are revising the schedule for major infrastructure projects, including the construction of new skyscrapers, in an effort to reduce their current spending

stories.title: Attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure may impact global economy, top diplomat warns
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi stressed that the aggressors will be solely responsible for this

stories.title: Diplomat says over 80 universities, libraries hit by US-Israeli strikes in Iran
stories.description: "Attacks on universities and research centers more than anything else expose the antihuman nature of Iran’s adversaries," Ambassador of the Islamic Republic in Tajikistan Alireza Haqiqian said

stories.title: CIA runs deception campaign to aid rescue of F-15E crew member in Iran — TV
stories.description: The F-15E two-seat fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday

stories.title: Iran condemns attack on its heavy water facility as assault on science
stories.description: Despite enemy strikes, "neither scientific progress nor the desire to serve peace and health globally will stop" in Iran, the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran stated

stories.title: SCO advocates for political, diplomatic settlement around Iran — secretary-general
stories.description: Nurlan Yermekbayev noted that the SCO issued a political statement expressing serious concern about the ongoing events

stories.title: US, Israel prepare to carry out massive strikes on Iranian energy facilities — media
stories.description: Donald Trump earlier threatened to target energy and civilian infrastructure in Iran unless Tehran reopened the Strait of Hormuz by April 6

stories.title: Six children killed in overnight strikes on Tehran — authorities
stories.description: The media also reported that a residential building in southeastern Tehran had been destroyed by a strike

stories.title: Trump doesn't rule out sending US troops to Iran — The Hill
stories.description: The US could consider striking any infrastructure facilities in Iran if there is no deal, the president noted

stories.title: US has already spent over $42 bln on military operation in Iran — portal
stories.description: The portal's calculations are based on an estimate stated during a Pentagon briefing for the US Congress on March 10

stories.title: Passage via Strait of Hormuz will never be the same, especially for US and Israel — IRGC
stories.description: The military say that the IRGC Navy is completing the correspondent operational preparations

stories.title: UK Navy reports critical situation in Persian Gulf region
stories.description: 27 attacks on ships and port infrastructure have been reported since March 1, and the number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz daily has dropped from 138 to eight

stories.title: UK Defense Ministry denies reports of its destroyer being hit off Lebanese coast
stories.description: Israel’s Channel 14 earlier cited assessments that a missile fired by Hezbollah was able to damage the ship

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
